message:
Update daemon API to return structured SelectionResponse with reasons

Changes the daemon's /select-worker endpoint to return informative
responses when no worker is available, instead of returning an error.
This enables graceful local fallback in the hook.

Changes:
- Check if any workers are configured before selection
  Returns NoWorkersConfigured if workers.toml is empty

- Check if any healthy workers exist before selection
  Returns AllWorkersUnreachable if all workers failed health checks

- Handle no available slots gracefully
  Returns AllWorkersBusy instead of error when all workers at capacity

- Handle slot reservation race conditions
  Returns AllWorkersBusy if slots claimed between selection and reservation

- Construct SelectedWorker with full details on success
  Returns worker: Some(SelectedWorker{...}), reason: Success

Benefits:
- Hook can distinguish between "no daemon" and "no workers available"
- Hook can show specific reason to user when falling back to local
- No HTTP 4xx errors for valid "no worker available" scenarios
- Better observability and debugging

Part of: remote_compilation_helper-4ur (Add reason field for no-worker cases)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
